summary.intent: Fix a fallback-ID bug in PR #667 of fogodev/ars-ui, then address a subsequent Codex review pass that surfaced four new findings: two Dioxus stale root-attribute reactivity bugs and two Leptos test feature-gate compile failures.
summary.outcome: All four Codex findings were resolved: Dioxus Button and Tabs components now include non-reactive props as explicit `use_reactive` dependencies so root attrs refresh on prop changes, and Leptos SSR tests were gated with the correct `#[cfg]` feature flags. Changes were committed as 'fix(adapters): refresh root attrs on prop changes' and pushed; all targeted tests, strict clippy, and coverage checks passed.

repo: The repo uses a Codex bot that posts review threads on PRs and signals state via GitHub reactions (👀 = active, +1 = approved); the workflow polls reactions + unresolved thread count to decide next action.
ars-dioxus and ars-leptos are separate crates in a Cargo workspace; Leptos tests require `--no-default-features --features ssr` to compile SSR-gated test targets.

Dioxus root-attr derivations in Button and Tabs used closures that captured prop values without declaring them as `use_reactive` dependencies, causing stale attrs when only props changed without machine-state changes.

A shared helper was introduced to seed reactive dependencies from borrowed prop attr slices, borrowing at the call boundary and cloning only to register the dependency — required to satisfy strict clippy (-D warnings).
CI pipeline includes multiple sequential check tiers: unit, feature-flag variants (Dioxus features), release, coverage — coverage is the last check to complete.

code.finding: Root-attr derivation closure for Button did not list prop attrs as `use_reactive` dependencies; fixed by passing prop attrs through the new helper so Dioxus tracks them as reactive inputs.
The as-child rendering path had the same stale-attr bug as the primary path and required the same `use_reactive` dependency fix.
Tabs root-attr derivation had the same missing `use_reactive` dependency pattern as Button.
Test file was missing a feature gate (`#[cfg(feature = "ssr")]` or similar) causing compile failure when run with `--no-default-features --features ssr`.
Same missing feature-gate issue as attr_passthrough.rs for the no-default SSR test configuration.

workflow: Regression tests were written before implementation changes to confirm the bug is reproducible and to guard against regressions — tests were verified to fail first, then pass after the fix.
Strict clippy (`-D warnings`) is enforced on test targets; a helper that accepted owned Vecs caused a clippy lint and had to be refactored to borrow at the boundary.

`use_reactive` in Dioxus 0.7.x requires explicit enumeration of all non-signal values that should trigger re-derivation; closures that close over props silently become stale if the props aren't listed as dependencies.
The Codex review loop used a ~2-minute polling interval checking reactions, unresolved thread count, and CI check rollup simultaneously to minimize API calls while staying responsive.

friction: Long polling wait (~30+ minutes of 120-second sleep cycles) between triggering the Codex review and receiving its verdict, with no push notification mechanism available.
Strict clippy on test targets required a non-obvious refactor of the new helper (borrow at boundary, clone only for reactive seeding) that was not apparent until after the initial implementation.
Four Codex findings arrived as a batch only after the full CI suite completed, meaning the fix-review-push cycle is inherently serial and slow.
Leptos test feature-gate failures were not caught by the default `cargo test` invocation, only surfaced when running with explicit `--no-default-features --features ssr` flags.
open_items: Coverage CI check was still running at the end of the prior Codex pass; its final result was not confirmed before moving to the next fix cycle.
No audit of other Dioxus components beyond Button and Tabs for the same missing `use_reactive` dependency pattern — other components may have the same stale-attr bug.
The Codex polling loop is manual and fragile; a webhook or GitHub Actions notification trigger would be more reliable than repeated sleep+poll cycles.
